title: 401 unauthorized on every API call after IdP cert rotation
description: Customer 'Helio Inc' reports all API calls returning 401 since 09:00. They rotated their Okta SAML cert at 08:55. Internal SDK is still validating against old JWKS cache.
gold_root_cause: Customer rotated their IdP SAML certificate but their internal SDK is caching JWKS for 24h. Stale cache rejects new token signatures.
gold_remediation: Customer admin must clear SDK JWKS cache or wait TTL. Educate on shorter cache TTL configuration.
resolution: Walked customer through cache flush. APIs accepting tokens within 10 minutes. SDK upgrade recommended to v3.4 which respects JWKS cache headers.

title: 429 rate-limit surge starting 17:48, ~120 in 10 minutes
description: VC-Observability alert TENANT-429-RATE fired at 17:51 with 429 count of 64 in last 10 minutes, climbing. Customer 'NorthRange Logistics' (TEN-NORTHRANGE-2023). Spread across multiple endpoints.
gold_root_cause: Customer-side retry storm. Their nightly batch job hit a transient 502 at 17:30, retry-without-backoff loop kicked in and saturated their per-tenant limit.
gold_remediation: Recommend exponential backoff and SDK upgrade. Confirm batch job pattern. No platform action.
resolution: Engaged customer engineering. Retry storm confirmed. Customer rolled out backoff fix. 429 rate normalised by 18:14. SDK upgrade ticket logged with customer.

title: Webhook delivery degradation, NACK rate 1.8% on primary pool
description: WHK-WORKER-LATENCY-HIGH fired at 14:08 on primary delivery pool. NACK rate climbed past 1% within 90 seconds. Standby pool healthy per linkhealth output. Status page shows no public incident yet.
gold_root_cause: Primary delivery pool degraded due to upstream worker capacity issue. Failover to standby authorised but blocked by expired TLS certificate on standby pool.
gold_remediation: Authorise failover to standby. If certificate fails, emergency rotation per RB-SEC-12.
resolution: Failover initiated 14:14. Cert expiry on standby blocked clean failover until 14:31. Backlog drained 14:38-15:00. PIR scheduled, see INC001.

title: WHK-RETRY-EXHAUSTED on single endpoint, customer Helio Inc
description: VC-Webhooks alert RETRY-EXHAUSTED fired on customer 'Helio Inc' (TEN-HELIO-2024). Single endpoint /webhooks/orders has been retrying for 24h on all 8 attempts. Endpoint returns 502.
gold_root_cause: Customer-side endpoint outage. Their staging endpoint was registered in production webhook config by mistake. Staging is down for the weekend.
gold_remediation: Customer admin updates endpoint URL to production. Replay-by-time once endpoint healthy. No platform action.
resolution: Customer engineering confirmed staging endpoint mistake. URL updated. Replay-by-time of last 24h queued. 142 events redelivered successfully.

title: User reports access to records they should not see, possible cross-tenant
description: Customer admin at 'Cordwell Pharma' (TEN-CORDWELL-2022) reports an internal user can see records labelled with another tenant's ID. Customer is concerned about cross-tenant exposure.
gold_root_cause: Service account configured legitimately across two tenants by the customer's parent company group structure. Records were correctly scoped, but UI displayed parent-group label which the user mistook for cross-tenant.
gold_remediation: Pull VC-Identity audit log. Confirm denial-or-grant pattern. Engage Privacy if any genuine cross-tenant access. Customer-facing language drafted by Privacy.
resolution: Audit log confirmed all access was within service-account-permitted scope. No cross-tenant exposure. UI label clarified with customer admin. Customer satisfied. Privacy notified for record-keeping but no action.

title: Cross-product sync lag climbing, real-time dashboards stale
description: VC-DATAPLATFORM-LATENCY-HIGH and SYNC-LAG-HIGH alerts active since 17:30. Sync lag CRM-to-Analytics climbing from 35s baseline towards 28 minutes peak. 47 enterprise tenants affected.
gold_root_cause: Sync queue back-pressure caused by VC-DataPlatform latency. Q1 reporting batch was running on live production cluster contrary to ringfencing policy.
gold_remediation: Scale sync worker pool per RB011. Investigate VC-DataPlatform batch contention. Notify VP Customer Support and DPO if SLA breach trajectory crosses threshold.
resolution: Worker pool scaled at 18:03. Reporting batch killed at 18:48. Lag back to baseline by 19:15. Near-miss on 24h-average SLA. PIR scheduled, see INC002.

title: Spike in WHK-SIG-MISMATCH errors, ~80 in 30 minutes, customer Northwave
description: VC-Webhooks alert WHK-SIG-MISMATCH-RATE fired at 08:55. Customer 'Northwave Tech' (TEN-NORTHWAVE-2025) seeing 80+ signature failures in 30 minutes. All other customers fine.
gold_root_cause: Customer rotated webhook signing secret in their VertexCloud admin console at 08:50 but did not push the new secret to their receiver application until 10:00. Mismatch window of 70 minutes.
gold_remediation: Confirm with customer admin. Recommend coordinated rotation procedure (overlap window, dual-secret support). No platform action.
resolution: Customer engineering confirmed rotation timing issue. New secret deployed at 09:38. Signature errors stopped at 09:42. Goodwill replay of failed events queued. Coordinated-rotation guide shared with customer.

title: Both API gateway regions elevated latency, ~280ms p95, no errors yet
description: VC-API-GATEWAY-LATENCY-HIGH fired on EU region at 13:44, then US region 90 seconds later. p95 on both ~280ms vs 80ms baseline. Error rate normal. No public incident on status page.
gold_root_cause: Cloud provider maintenance event affecting both regions of VertexCloud's underlying CDN. Provider confirmed 12 minutes after symptom start. Resolved by them.
gold_remediation: Hold; communicate via incident bridge. VertexCloud cannot remediate provider directly. Customer Success prep proactive comms language for affected customers.
resolution: Cloud provider confirmed maintenance event at 13:56, completed at 14:08. Latency normalised by 14:18. No customer-impacting errors. Proactive comms not sent.

title: Multiple customer reports of duplicate records in Analytics Cloud after SDK upgrade
description: Contact Centre escalated 6 customer reports in 90 minutes, all from customers running SDK v4.18, all reporting duplicate rows in Analytics Cloud target. VC-Sync dedup engine did not catch any.
gold_root_cause: SDK v4.18 introduced bug where dedup-key was regenerated on retry rather than reused. Different keys, identical content. VC-Sync dedup engine could not detect.
gold_remediation: Page Data Platform Engineering. Hotfix or roll back SDK release. Reconciliation team de-duplicates affected records. Authorisation: Reconciliation Manager.
resolution: Engineering rolled back SDK v4.18 to v4.17.3 at 16:02. Reconciliation processed for 6 reported customers plus 9 more identified post-rollback. Total 15 customers affected, all de-duplicated within 24 hours.

title: Customer integration broken, 403 PERMISSION_DENIED on /v2/cases endpoint
description: Customer 'Stagecoach Retail' (TEN-STAGECOACH-2021) reports their case-creation integration started returning 403 today at 09:00. Single endpoint, single integration.
gold_root_cause: Customer admin removed 'cases:write' scope from the OAuth client used by the integration during a routine permissions audit on 03-12. Did not inform integration owner.
gold_remediation: Standard customer comms. Customer admin re-grants scope. No retry needed once scope restored.
resolution: Customer admin re-granted scope at 11:30. Integration recovered. Communicated permission-audit-coordination guidance to customer.

title: Customer receiving 410 Gone on /v1/records endpoint after v1 sunset
description: Customer 'Bluebell Analytics' (TEN-BLUEBELL-2023) reports all /v1/records calls returning 410 since 09:00. Customer was notified of v1 sunset in January but did not migrate.
gold_root_cause: Customer did not migrate from deprecated v1 API before sunset date of 2026-03-14. 410 Gone is expected and correct per deprecation policy.
gold_remediation: Provide v2 migration guide. Offer 30-day extended access window only if on enterprise contract and executive approval obtained.
resolution: Customer did not have enterprise contract. Provided v2 migration guide. Customer migrated within 6 hours.

title: Payload shape mismatch after SDK upgrade — missing 'metadata' field
description: Customer 'Orion Logistics' (TEN-ORION-2024) reports their parser is breaking after upgrading from SDK v3.x to v4.x. The 'metadata' field is no longer nested under 'record'.
gold_root_cause: v4 API schema moved 'metadata' from nested under 'record' to top-level. This is a documented breaking change in the v3→v4 migration guide. Customer did not read migration notes.
gold_remediation: Share migration guide section on schema changes. Customer updates parser. No platform action.
resolution: Customer updated parser to reference top-level metadata. Integration restored.

title: SDK v4.21 throws AttributeError on client.cases.list() — regression
description: Customer 'Meridian Tech' (TEN-MERIDIAN-2025) upgraded to SDK v4.21 and now gets AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'data' on cases.list(). Downgrade to v4.20 resolves.
gold_root_cause: SDK v4.21 introduced a regression where list response pagination wrapper returns None instead of an empty list when result set is empty. Downgrade to v4.20 confirms.
gold_remediation: Advise customer to stay on v4.20 until patch is released. Escalate to SDK team for hotfix.
resolution: SDK team confirmed regression. Patch v4.21.1 released within 4 hours. Customer upgraded and confirmed resolution.

title: SDK initialisation failing — NoneType on client.api_key
description: Customer 'Cascade Group' (TEN-CASCADE-2022) reports SDK failing on import. Stack trace shows NoneType on client.api_key at init time. Environment variable VC_API_KEY is set in their CI.
gold_root_cause: Customer's CI pipeline sets VC_API_KEY after SDK import in their bootstrap script order. SDK reads the env var at import time, not lazily.
gold_remediation: Customer must set environment variables before importing or instantiating the SDK client. Show bootstrap order fix.
resolution: Customer fixed bootstrap order. SDK initialises cleanly.

title: SSL: WRONG_VERSION_NUMBER after customer infrastructure migration
description: Customer 'Pellucid Data' (TEN-PELLUCID-2024) migrated their on-prem infrastructure to a new host last night. SDK now throws 'SSL: WRONG_VERSION_NUMBER' on all API calls.
gold_root_cause: New host has Python runtime linked against OpenSSL 1.0.x which does not support TLS 1.2+. VertexCloud gateway rejects TLS 1.0/1.1 connections.
gold_remediation: Customer must upgrade OpenSSL on the new host to 1.1.1+ or switch to a runtime with updated SSL support.
resolution: Customer upgraded to Python 3.11 with bundled OpenSSL 3.x. SSL errors resolved.

title: Bulk export job stuck in PROCESSING for 90 minutes, no webhook
description: Customer 'Apex Financial' (TEN-APEX-2021) submitted a bulk export job at 10:00. Status endpoint shows PROCESSING after 90 minutes — baseline for this job size is 8 minutes. No completion webhook fired.
gold_root_cause: Job worker OOMKilled at 10:18 mid-processing. Kubernetes restarted the pod but the job did not re-queue automatically due to a missing restart policy. Job left as zombie in PROCESSING state.
gold_remediation: Tier 3 to manually re-queue job via internal tooling. Platform Engineering to fix restart policy. Notify customer.
resolution: Job manually re-queued at 12:20. Completed at 12:43. Completion webhook fired successfully. Platform Engineering added pod restart auto-requeue in hotfix.

title: Duplicate records after customer retried failed import job without idempotency key
description: Customer 'Solent Retail' (TEN-SOLENT-2023) ran a bulk import, got a 502, retried — now has ~4,000 duplicate records. Did not pass idempotency_key.
gold_root_cause: Customer retried a partial-success import without an idempotency key. Both the original and the retry partially succeeded, creating duplicates for the overlapping records.
gold_remediation: Provide SQL query to identify duplicates by (external_id, created_at window). Customer reconciles. Educate on idempotency_key usage.
resolution: Customer identified and removed 3,941 duplicate records using provided query. Shared idempotency key documentation.

title: Incremental sync completing but data not appearing in Analytics target
description: Customer 'Thornfield Media' (TEN-THORNFIELD-2024) reports sync jobs completing (200 OK, webhook fires) but Analytics Cloud dashboard not reflecting new data.
gold_root_cause: Analytics Cloud dashboard has a 15-minute refresh cache. Data was synced correctly — customer was checking the dashboard before refresh window elapsed.
gold_remediation: Explain the 15-minute cache behaviour. Customer can trigger manual refresh via Analytics dashboard settings.
resolution: Customer confirmed data appeared after 15 minutes. Cache behaviour documented and link sent.

title: Mass SSL cert verify failed across multiple enterprise customers — post-rotation
description: Contact Centre received 12 escalations in 20 minutes at 09:05. All customers report SSL certificate verify failed starting 08:55. Correlates with VertexCloud scheduled cert rotation at 08:50.
gold_root_cause: Multiple enterprise customers had implemented certificate pinning. VertexCloud rotated the leaf certificate as scheduled. Pinned-cert customers immediately failed.
gold_remediation: Identify all certificate-pinning customers from the registry. Contact each. Provide new cert fingerprint. Strongly advise against certificate pinning — use CA pinning instead.
resolution: 11 of 12 customers updated pinned certs within 2 hours. 1 required 4h for internal approval. All recovered. Post-incident comms sent. Certificate pinning guidance added to onboarding docs.

title: Webhook delivery failing CERT_EXPIRED to customer endpoint
description: VC-Webhooks alert CERT-EXPIRED on TEN-KELPEX-2022 (Kelpex Corp) at 13:12. Delivery to https://hooks.kelpex.com/vc failing with SSL certificate expired error.
gold_root_cause: Customer's webhook endpoint SSL certificate expired at 12:00 today. Delivery failure is on the customer's side.
gold_remediation: Notify customer to renew their endpoint certificate. Offer HTTP fallback for up to 48h on P1 request if customer cannot renew immediately.
resolution: Customer renewed certificate at 13:58. Webhook deliveries resumed. Backlog of 47 events replayed.

title: mTLS auth failures after customer certificate renewal — wrong chain
description: Customer 'Hargreave Financial' (TEN-HARGREAVE-2025) renewed their mTLS client certificate yesterday but auth calls now return certificate_required. Certificate upload confirmed in VertexCloud admin.
gold_root_cause: Customer uploaded only the leaf certificate without the intermediate CA chain. VertexCloud mTLS validation requires the full chain. Previous cert happened to have the same intermediate in trust store; new cert uses a different intermediate.
gold_remediation: Customer must upload the full certificate chain (leaf + intermediate + root) in PEM bundle format.
resolution: Customer re-uploaded full chain in correct PEM bundle format. mTLS auth restored at 11:18.

title: API response times > 8s, all endpoints, all tenants — DB pool exhaustion
description: VC-API-GATEWAY-LATENCY-HIGH alert at 14:10. p95 latency climbing: 2s at 14:10, 5s at 14:15, 8s at 14:20. Error rate starting to climb at 14:20. All tenants affected. SLA breach imminent.
gold_root_cause: Long-running analytics query from a single enterprise tenant held 340 of 350 available DB connections for 40 minutes. Triggered by their scheduled quarter-end reporting at 13:30. Query was running on production cluster rather than the analytics replica.
gold_remediation: Terminate long-running query (DBA action). Scale connection pool ceiling. Route analytics queries to read replica.
resolution: DBA terminated offending queries at 14:28. API latency normalised by 14:45. Connection pool ceiling increased to 500 as interim measure. Analytics replica routing enforcement deployed next day.

title: Connection pool timeout errors on Analytics Cloud, single region
description: VC-DATAPLATFORM alert CONNECTION-POOL-TIMEOUT fired in eu-west-1 at 09:30. Errors visible to customers in EU region only. US region healthy.
gold_root_cause: EU region suffered a thundering herd of reconnections after a brief 90-second DB availability blip at 09:15. All services reconnected simultaneously, saturating the pool ceiling.
gold_remediation: Wait for thundering herd to self-resolve with exponential backoff. If not resolving in 5 min, engage Platform Engineering for controlled traffic shedding.
resolution: Self-resolved within 4 minutes as backoff spread connections. Errors cleared by 09:34. No customer-facing SLA breach.

title: API p95 latency 4.2s, climbing — started at deploy 10:55
description: VC-API-GATEWAY-LATENCY-HIGH fired at 11:00. Latency spike correlates exactly with the 10:55 deploy of gateway v2.14.1. p95 at 4.2s vs 300ms baseline. Error rate stable but latency rising.
gold_root_cause: v2.14.1 introduced N+1 query pattern in the request authorization middleware. Every API call now makes one DB call per permission scope instead of a batched lookup. High-scope tokens hit 12+ DB calls per request.
gold_remediation: Roll back v2.14.1 immediately. Fix N+1 pattern and re-deploy with batched authorization lookup.
resolution: Rollback to v2.13.9 completed at 11:22. Latency normalised by 11:24. Fix deployed as v2.14.2 the following day.

title: Single tenant reporting p95 > 3s on /v2/contacts/search endpoint only
description: Customer 'Irongate Partners' (TEN-IRONGATE-2023) reporting very slow search since 15:00. Other endpoints normal. Other tenants not affected on same endpoint.
gold_root_cause: Customer's contacts table grew to 2.4M records. Query is performing a full table scan because the customer's custom field used in their search filter is not indexed. Issue is tenant-specific.
gold_remediation: Add index on the customer's custom field — requires Platform Engineering and customer approval. Short-term: advise customer to narrow search filter to reduce scan scope.
resolution: Customer narrowed filter as interim fix. Platform Engineering added index on custom field during next maintenance window. Search latency normalised.

title: Customer reports latency spikes every 30 seconds on Analytics API
description: Customer 'Whitstable Commerce' (TEN-WHITSTABLE-2022) reports periodic latency spikes exactly every 30 seconds. Between spikes responses are fast.
gold_root_cause: GC pauses on the Analytics API worker pods due to heap pressure from large in-memory dataset caching. 30-second GC interval matches Java G1GC configuration on this service.
gold_remediation: Platform Engineering to tune JVM heap and GC configuration. Short-term customer impact is acceptable (< 2s spikes). No customer action needed.
resolution: Notified customer of platform-side GC tuning in progress. Platform Engineering adjusted heap size and GC params. Spikes resolved in next deployment cycle.

title: 503 Service Unavailable, all customers, eu-west-1 region
description: Multiple customer-facing alerts plus status page auto-trigger at 07:15. VC-API-Gateway in eu-west-1 returning 503 on all endpoints. US region healthy.
gold_root_cause: EU region node failure removed 6 of 8 pods from load balancer rotation simultaneously. Remaining 2 pods unable to handle traffic load. Load balancer health checks failed. Root cause: underlying cloud provider node failure.
gold_remediation: Platform Engineering to scale up replacement pods in EU region. Cloud provider to investigate node failure.
resolution: 5 replacement pods launched by 07:28. Full capacity restored by 07:35. 503s stopped at 07:35. Total duration 20 minutes. Post-incident action: increase minimum pod count in EU from 2 to 4.

title: Flapping 503s, 10-20s intervals, correlating with rolling deploy
description: VC-API-GATEWAY-ERROR-RATE-HIGH fired at 13:05. 503 errors lasting 15-20 seconds then clearing, then returning. Pattern started with rolling deploy of v2.15.0 at 13:00.
gold_root_cause: Zero-downtime rolling deploy misconfiguration: maxUnavailable set to 50% means during rollout half the pods are unavailable. Traffic spike during deploy window overwhelmed remaining pods.
gold_remediation: Pause rollout. Fix maxUnavailable to 25% and maxSurge to 50%. Resume rollout.
resolution: Rollout paused at 13:12. Config fixed and rollout resumed at 13:18. Completed without further 503s by 13:28.

title: Consumer lag growing on order-events topic, 80k messages behind
description: VC-DATAPLATFORM alert CONSUMER-LAG-HIGH at 10:30 on order-events topic. Lag at 80k messages and growing at 5k/min. Consumer group vc-order-processor has 0 active consumers.
gold_root_cause: All consumer pods in vc-order-processor group crashed at 10:25 due to OOMKill. A schema change deployed at 10:20 caused all messages to fail deserialization, triggering retry storms that exhausted pod memory.
gold_remediation: Roll back the schema change. Restart consumer pods. Process DLQ messages after schema rollback. Investigate schema compatibility violation.
resolution: Schema rolled back at 10:55. Consumer pods restarted and healthy by 11:05. Lag drained by 11:40. 2,341 messages in DLQ inspected — all recoverable after schema fix.

title: Customer subscription paused — quota exceeded on analytics-events stream
description: Customer 'Fenwick Digital' (TEN-FENWICK-2024) reports their analytics event subscription has been auto-paused. Alert: SUBSCRIPTION-PAUSED-QUOTA-EXCEEDED.
gold_root_cause: Customer's event subscription processed 8.2M events in March against a 5M/month quota on their Growth tier. Auto-pause triggered at quota ceiling.
gold_remediation: Discuss tier upgrade with TAM. Short-term: TAM can grant a one-time 30-day quota extension with VP CS approval. Subscription can be manually unpaused once upgrade is confirmed.
resolution: TAM approved quota extension pending tier upgrade discussion. Subscription unpaused. Customer confirmed intent to upgrade to Enterprise tier.

title: Customer reports out-of-order events on orders stream after failover
description: Customer 'Cavendish Retail' (TEN-CAVENDISH-2021) reports their order processing is failing with sequence errors since the region failover event yesterday. Events arriving out of expected order.
gold_root_cause: During the failover event, partition leadership was reassigned. Some messages from the pre-failover leader were delivered after messages from the new leader, breaking ordering guarantees within a partition window.
gold_remediation: Customer must implement idempotent, sequence-tolerant event processing. Platform cannot guarantee ordering across failover windows. Provide guidance on sequence-number-based deduplication.
resolution: Customer engineering implemented sequence-number deduplication. Backlog reprocessed cleanly. Ordering issue cannot recur with this pattern.

title: Customer receiving stale dashboard data 40 minutes after record update
description: Customer 'Silverstone Analytics' (TEN-SILVERSTONE-2023) reports updating a record via API then seeing the old value in Analytics Cloud dashboard 40 minutes later. Expected freshness: < 5 minutes.
gold_root_cause: CDN configuration change deployed at 10:00 set Cache-Control max-age to 3600s on Analytics API responses, replacing the correct 300s. Customer is hitting a cached response.
gold_remediation: Platform Engineering to correct CDN configuration. Purge cached responses for affected endpoints. Revert to 300s max-age.
resolution: CDN config corrected at 11:48. Cache purged for Analytics API endpoints. Customer confirmed data freshness restored.

title: Customer reports latency spike for ~10s after cache flush, now resolved
description: Customer 'Peregrine Systems' (TEN-PEREGRINE-2022) reports API calls slowed to ~4s for about 10 seconds at 15:20, then recovered. Happened immediately after they cleared their CRM cache via admin settings.
gold_root_cause: Customer triggered a full cache flush for their tenant. This caused a cache stampede — all subsequent requests hit origin simultaneously. Cache repopulated within ~10 seconds. Expected behaviour.
gold_remediation: Explain cache stampede behaviour. Recommend using targeted cache invalidation rather than full flush where possible. No platform action needed.
resolution: Customer acknowledged behaviour. Advised on partial cache invalidation options.

title: EU customers seeing data written in US — cross-region replication lag 45 minutes
description: EU-based enterprise customers reporting data written in US region visible in EU but with 45-minute delay. Replication lag alert fired at 08:30. Affects all EU enterprise tenants.
gold_root_cause: Network partition between US-EAST-1 and EU-WEST-1 at 08:00 caused replication to pause. Partition resolved at 08:30 but lag accumulated during the 30-minute partition window is draining.
gold_remediation: Monitor lag drainage — should self-resolve as replication catches up. Throttle non-critical write traffic to EU to allow faster catch-up. Notify EU enterprise TAMs.
resolution: Lag drained to baseline (< 5s) by 09:40. EU TAMs notified 10 enterprise customers proactively. No data loss confirmed.

title: Customer hitting old region IP after failover — ECONNREFUSED
description: Customer 'Denholm Consulting' (TEN-DENHOLM-2024) reports ECONNREFUSED on all API calls since the region failover at 12:00. Other customers on same region are healthy.
gold_root_cause: Customer's internal DNS resolver has a 4-hour TTL. VertexCloud reduced DNS TTL to 60s for failover support, but the customer's resolver is ignoring the TTL and serving the old IP.
gold_remediation: Customer must flush their DNS cache or wait for their resolver TTL to expire. Advise on DNS cache flush procedure for their OS/resolver.
resolution: Customer flushed DNS cache at 14:35. API calls restored. Recommended customer configure resolver to respect returned TTLs.

title: Customer locked out after admin accidentally removed SSO configuration
description: Customer admin 'Hartwell Systems' (TEN-HARTWELL-2023) reports all users unable to log in. Admin accidentally removed their SSO configuration while exploring settings.
gold_root_cause: Customer admin deleted the active SSO SAML configuration. All users relying on SAML auth are now blocked. VertexCloud fallback to email/password auth is available but customers were never provisioned with passwords.
gold_remediation: Restore SSO config from audit log backup (Tier 3 action). Provision temporary password-reset tokens for admin users so they can regain access while SSO is restored.
resolution: SSO configuration restored from audit log by Tier 3 at 10:35. Admin users sent password-reset tokens as fallback. SSO confirmed working at 10:42.

title: OAuth token refresh loop — customer integration hitting rate limit on /auth/token
description: Customer 'Bracken Tech' (TEN-BRACKEN-2025) integration is being rate-limited on the token endpoint. Alert WHK-RATE-LIMIT fired. Their integration is calling /auth/token every 10 seconds.
gold_root_cause: Customer integration code has a bug: it refreshes the OAuth token on every API call rather than only when the token is expired. Token lifetime is 1 hour; they are refreshing 360x per token lifetime.
gold_remediation: Customer must implement token caching. Share code snippet showing correct refresh pattern (check expiry before refresh, use token until 5 minutes before expiry).
resolution: Customer fixed token caching logic. Token endpoint rate limiting cleared within 2 minutes. Integration stable.

title: New user cannot see any records despite correct role assignment
description: Customer 'Langdale Group' (TEN-LANGDALE-2022) reports a new user account assigned the 'Sales Manager' role cannot see any CRM records. Role was assigned 30 minutes ago.
gold_root_cause: Role assignment permissions propagate asynchronously with up to a 15-minute delay due to permission cache TTL. User was checking access within the propagation window.
gold_remediation: Advise customer to wait up to 15 minutes for permission propagation. No platform action needed. Document this behaviour in onboarding materials.
resolution: Customer confirmed user access resolved after 12 minutes. Propagation delay documented and shared with customer.

title: Sync lag alert — 18-minute lag on CRM-to-Analytics for tenant Kestrel
description: SYNC-LAG-HIGH alert fired for TEN-KESTREL-2023 at 13:45. Lag at 18 minutes and not draining. Single-tenant impact.
gold_root_cause: Customer's CRM tenant has 14M contact records. A schema migration on their tenant at 13:00 triggered a full re-index of their contact table, flooding the sync queue with change events from 14M records simultaneously.
gold_remediation: Throttle the change-event flood from the re-index to protect sync queue. Platform Engineering to apply per-tenant event rate limit during migrations. Lag will drain within 2 hours under throttle.
resolution: Platform Engineering applied throttle at 14:05. Lag began draining steadily. Returned to baseline at 15:55. Post-incident: full re-index events should bypass the real-time sync queue and use the bulk sync path.

title: Webhook delivery NACK rate 2.1% — payload signature mismatch on new signing key rollout
description: WHK-NACK-RATE-HIGH alert at 10:30. NACK rate 2.1% on EU delivery pool. Correlates with webhook signing key rotation at 10:20 that was applied to EU pool first.
gold_root_cause: Webhook signing key rotation applied to EU delivery pool before customers were notified to update their signature verification keys. Customers validating with old key reject payloads signed with new key during the transition window.
gold_remediation: Dual-sign webhooks with both old and new key during a 24h transition window. Notify customers of new key. Remove old key after 24h.
resolution: Dual-signing activated at 10:45. NACK rate dropped to 0.1% within 10 minutes as customers accepted either signature. Customers notified of new key via email + webhook header.
